milestoneTitle: Phase M9.4b — fix: swipe-left gesture wasn't firing on iOS because Safari cancels pointer events on a touch-action: pan-y element as soon as the gesture commits horizontal. Rewired wireLearnRowGestures with a dual-path handler: touchstart/touchmove/touchend (passive: false on move) calls preventDefault() the first frame we lock horizontal, which keeps the drag alive; pointerdown/move/up retained for desktop mouse. Shared state machine with a horizontalLocked flag so we only block vertical scroll once the user has clearly committed left.
